title: Feature, quality attribute, or constraint?
intro: Sort each requirement. The distinction decides what your architecture must be designed around.

cards.text: Users can reset their password by email
Show order history with filtering and search
p99 API latency under 200 ms at 10k requests/sec
Handle a 10× traffic spike without manual intervention
EU user data must never leave EU data centres (GDPR)
Must use the bank's mandated KYC vendor

debrief: Features describe *what* the system does — and almost any architecture can deliver them, so they don't drive design. Quality attributes are measurable *how-well* targets (availability, latency, scalability) — these are the primary architectural drivers. Constraints are external, unnegotiable limits (regulation, a mandated vendor) you must design around. Notice the quality attributes are all numbers — that's the test of a good one.
